**Justification:**

**Observation Accuracy: mostly_accurate**
- The report correctly identifies most working capabilities (multi-source RSS, timezone conversion, date formatting, etc.)
- However, it incorrectly claims "deduplicating by timestamp ID" and "Incremental category updates" - the actual system overwrites files completely each run and has collision risks rather than true deduplication
- The cognitive framework (perceive/cache/filter/attend/remember/consolidate) is somewhat forced but generally maps correctly

**Gap Coverage:**
- ✅ gap_1 (HTTP error handling): Identified in Triage #4 "Perceive has no retry/timeout" and Triage #1 mentions "URL reachability"
- ✅ gap_2 (feed validation): Identified in Triage #1 "no validation of feed content"
- ✅ gap_3 (deduplication): Identified in Triage #3 "Timestamp-based IDs will collide"
- ✅ gap_4 (content extraction): Identified in Triage #10 "No entry content - Only captures title/link"
- ✅ gap_5 (caching/conditional fetching): Identified in Triage #6 "no incremental updates, no 'since last run' queries"
- ✅ gap_6 (rate limiting): Identified in Triage #9 "No rate limiting"
- ✅ gap_7 (persistent state): Identified in Triage #2 "Remember lacks read path - each run starts from scratch"
- ✅ gap_8 (error recovery): Identified in Triage #4 "Network failures cause silent data loss or full system exit"
- ❌ gap_9 (OPML/feed discovery): Not mentioned at all
- ❌ gap_10 (output format flexibility): Not mentioned at all

**Plan Specificity:**
- All 8 identified gaps have concrete, actionable plans with code examples showing exactly what to change
- Gaps 9 and 10 are absent from both triage and plan sections
